title: Alias pruning and canonical feature names
summary: Test a compact preprocessing variant that removes redundant aliases from otherwise similar feature families.
rationale: The export contained many near-duplicate feature blocks and identical prediction signals. Stronger ideas often came from the same mechanism rather than from many alternative names for the same quantity.
implementation_hint: For each mechanism, keep one canonical continuous margin, one normalized ratio, and one or two binary threshold flags. Drop duplicate aliases, repeated interaction names, debug/proxy metric columns, and overly similar per-compound summaries unless they capture distinct information.
expected_effect: Small validation-stability gain with medium confidence; may reduce overfit and runtime without changing the core signal.
risk: Removing aliases can hurt if AutoGluon benefited from redundant representations for different model types; test as a single compactness experiment.
